1369	    fn tuple<'a>(&mut self, elements: &'a [UntypedExpr], location: &SrcSpan) -> Document<'a> {
1370	        if elements.is_empty() {
1371	            // We take all comments that come _before_ the end of the tuple,
1372	            // that is all comments that are inside "#(" and ")", if there's
1373	            // any comment we want to put it inside the empty tuple!

1375	                None => "#()".to_doc(),

1384	                    //     force the breaks to be split on newlines.
1385	                    .force_break(),
1386	            };
1387	        }

1389	        self.append_inlinable_wrapped_arguments(
1390	            "#".to_doc(),
1391	            elements,
1392	            location,
1393	            |e| e,
1394	            |self_, e| self_.comma_separated_item(e, elements.len()),
1395	        )
1396	    }
1397	
1398	    // Appends to the given docs a comma-separated list of documents wrapped by
1399	    // parentheses. If the last item of the argument list is splittable the
1400	    // resulting document will try to first split that before splitting all the
1401	    // other arguments.
1402	    // This is used for function calls and tuples.
1403	    fn append_inlinable_wrapped_arguments<'a, 'b, T, ToExpr, ToDoc>(
1404	        &mut self,
1405	        doc: Document<'a>,
1406	        values: &'b [T],
1407	        location: &SrcSpan,
1408	        to_expr: ToExpr,
1409	        to_doc: ToDoc,
1410	    ) -> Document<'a>
1411	    where
1412	        T: HasLocation,
1413	        T: std::fmt::Debug,
1414	        ToExpr: Fn(&T) -> &UntypedExpr,
1415	        ToDoc: Fn(&mut Self, &'b T) -> Document<'a>,
1416	    {
1417	        match init_and_last(values) {
1418	            Some((initial_values, last_value))
1419	                if is_breakable_argument(to_expr(last_value), values.len())
1420	                    && !self.any_comments(last_value.location().start)
1421	                    && !self.any_comment_between(last_value.location().end, location.end) =>
1422	            {
1423	                let mut docs = initial_values
1424	                    .iter()
1425	                    .map(|value| to_doc(self, value))
1426	                    .collect_vec();
1427	
1428	                let last_value_doc = to_doc(self, last_value)
1429	                    .group()
1430	                    .next_break_fits(NextBreakFitsMode::Enabled);
1431	
1432	                docs.append(&mut vec![last_value_doc]);
1433	
1434	                doc.append(self.wrap_function_call_arguments(docs, location))
1435	                    .next_break_fits(NextBreakFitsMode::Disabled)
1436	                    .group()
1437	            }
1438	
1439	            Some(_) | None => {
1440	                let docs = values.iter().map(|value| to_doc(self, value)).collect_vec();
1441	                doc.append(self.wrap_function_call_arguments(docs, location))
1442	                    .group()
1443	            }
1444	        }
1445	    }
1446	
1447	    pub fn case<'a>(
1448	        &mut self,
1449	        subjects: &'a [UntypedExpr],
1450	        clauses: &'a [UntypedClause],
1451	        location: &'a SrcSpan,
1452	    ) -> Document<'a> {
1453	        let subjects_doc = break_("case", "case ")
1454	            .append(join(
1455	                subjects.iter().map(|s| self.expr(s).group()),
1456	                break_(",", ", "),
1457	            ))
1458	            .nest(INDENT)
1459	            .append(break_("", " "))
1460	            .append("{")
1461	            .next_break_fits(NextBreakFitsMode::Disabled)
1462	            .group();
1463	
1464	        let clauses_doc = concat(
1465	            clauses
1466	                .iter()
1467	                .enumerate()
1468	                .map(|(i, c)| self.clause(c, i as u32).group()),
1469	        );
1470	
1471	        // We get all remaining comments that come before the case's closing
1472	        // bracket. If there's any we add those before the closing bracket
1473	        // instead of moving those out of the case expression.
1474	        // Otherwise those would be moved out of the case expression.
1475	        let comments = self.pop_comments(location.end);
1476	        let closing_bracket = match printed_comments(comments, false) {
1477	            None => docvec![line(), "}"],
1478	            Some(comment) => docvec![line(), comment]
1479	                .nest(INDENT)
1480	                .append(line())
1481	                .append("}"),
1482	        };
1483	
1484	        subjects_doc
1485	            .append(line().append(clauses_doc).nest(INDENT))
1486	            .append(closing_bracket)
1487	            .force_break()
1488	    }
1489	
1490	    pub fn record_update<'a>(
1491	        &mut self,
1492	        constructor: &'a UntypedExpr,
1493	        record: &'a RecordBeingUpdated<UntypedExpr>,
1494	        arguments: &'a [UntypedRecordUpdateArg],
1495	        location: &SrcSpan,
1496	    ) -> Document<'a> {
1497	        let constructor_doc: Document<'a> = self.expr(constructor);
1498	        let pieces = std::iter::once(UntypedRecordUpdatePiece::Record(record))
1499	            .chain(arguments.iter().map(UntypedRecordUpdatePiece::Argument))
1500	            .collect_vec();
1501	
1502	        self.append_inlinable_wrapped_arguments(
1503	            constructor_doc,
1504	            &pieces,
1505	            location,
1506	            |arg| match arg {
1507	                UntypedRecordUpdatePiece::Argument(arg) => &arg.value,
1508	                UntypedRecordUpdatePiece::Record(record) => record.base.as_ref(),
1509	            },
1510	            |this, arg| match arg {
1511	                UntypedRecordUpdatePiece::Argument(arg) => this.record_update_arg(arg),
1512	                UntypedRecordUpdatePiece::Record(record) => {
1513	                    let comments = this.pop_comments(record.location.start);
1514	                    commented("..".to_doc().append(this.expr(&record.base)), comments)
1515	                }
1516	            },
1517	        )
1518	    }
1519	
1520	    pub fn const_record_update<'a, A, B>(
1521	        &mut self,
1522	        module: &Option<(EcoString, SrcSpan)>,
1523	        name: &'a EcoString,
1524	        record: &'a RecordBeingUpdated<Constant<A, B>>,
1525	        arguments: &'a [RecordUpdateArg<Constant<A, B>>],
1526	        location: &SrcSpan,
1527	    ) -> Document<'a> {
1528	        let constructor_doc = match module {
1529	            Some((m, _)) => m.to_doc().append(".").append(name.as_str()),
1530	            None => name.to_doc(),
1531	        };
1532	
1533	        let pieces = std::iter::once(RecordUpdatePiece::Record(record))
1534	            .chain(arguments.iter().map(RecordUpdatePiece::Argument))
1535	            .collect_vec();
1536	
1537	        let docs = pieces
1538	            .iter()
1539	            .map(|piece| match piece {
1540	                RecordUpdatePiece::Argument(arg) => {
1541	                    let comments = self.pop_comments(arg.location.start);
1542	                    let doc = match arg {
1543	                        _ if arg.uses_label_shorthand() => arg.label.as_str().to_doc().append(":"),
1544	                        _ => arg
1545	                            .label
1546	                            .as_str()
1547	                            .to_doc()
1548	                            .append(": ")
1549	                            .append(self.const_expr(&arg.value))
1550	                            .group(),
1551	                    };
1552	                    commented(doc, comments)
1553	                }
1554	                RecordUpdatePiece::Record(record) => {
1555	                    let comments = self.pop_comments(record.location.start);
1556	                    commented(
1557	                        "..".to_doc().append(self.const_expr(&record.base)),
1558	                        comments,
1559	                    )
1560	                }
1561	            })
1562	            .collect_vec();
1563	
1564	        constructor_doc
1565	            .append(self.wrap_arguments(docs, location.end))
1566	            .group()
1567	    }
1568	
1569	    pub fn bin_op<'a>(
1570	        &mut self,
1571	        name: &'a BinOp,
1572	        left: &'a UntypedExpr,
1573	        right: &'a UntypedExpr,
1574	        nest_steps: bool,
1575	    ) -> Document<'a> {
1576	        let left_side = self.bin_op_side(name, left, nest_steps);
1577	
1578	        let comments = self.pop_comments(right.start_byte_index());
1579	        let name_doc = break_("", " ").append(commented(name.to_doc(), comments));
1580	
1581	        let right_side = self.bin_op_side(name, right, nest_steps);
1582	
1583	        left_side
1584	            .append(if nest_steps {
1585	                name_doc.nest(INDENT)
1586	            } else {
1587	                name_doc
1588	            })
1589	            .append(" ")

content.text: I found the tuple formatting path: expression tuples use `append_inlinable_wrapped_arguments`, which treats a breakable final argument specially; that appears to be why the second nested tuple splits independently instead of causing the outer tuple to split like constructor calls. I’m adding a focused temporary regression test now.

name = "gleam-core"
version = "1.15.1"
authors = ["Louis Pilfold <louis@lpil.uk>"]
edition = "2024"
license = "Apache-2.0"

[dependencies]
# Error message and warning formatting
codespan-reporting = "0"
# Graph data structures
petgraph = "0.8"
# Template rendering
askama = "0"
# Markdown parsing
pulldown-cmark = { version = "0", default-features = false, features = [
  "html",
] }
# XDG directory locations
dirs-next = "2"
# SPDX license parsing
spdx = "0"
# Binary format de-serialization
bincode = { version = "2", features = ["alloc", "serde"] }
# cross platform single glob and glob set matching
globset = { version = "0", features = ["serde1"] }
# Checksums
xxhash-rust = { version = "0", features = ["xxh3"] }
# Pubgrub dependency resolution algorithm
pubgrub = "0.3"
# Used for converting absolute path to relative path
pathdiff = { version = "0", features = ["camino"] }
# Memory arena using ids rather than references
id-arena = "2"
# Unicode grapheme traversal
unicode-segmentation = "1.12.0"
# Bijective (bi-directional) hashmap
bimap = "0.6.3"
# Parsing of arbitrary width int values
num-bigint = { version = "0.4.6", features = ["serde"] }
num-traits = "0.2.19"
# Encryption
age = { version = "0.11", features = ["armor"] }
radix_trie = "0.3"
# Ensuring recursive type-checking doesn't stack overflow
stacker = "0.1.21"
# Manipulating bit arrays
bitvec = { version = "1", features = ["serde"] }

async-trait.workspace = true

content.text:
Implemented the formatter fix in `compiler-core/src/format.rs`.

What changed:
- Tuple formatting now avoids the special “inline last splittable argument” behavior when the last element is itself a tuple.
- This lets nested tuple arguments break at the outer tuple level, matching constructor-style formatting, instead of keeping the outer tuple inline and only breaking the final nested tuple.

Validation:
- Added a temporary regression test reproducing the nested tuple formatting issue.
- Confirmed it failed before/without the correct behavior and passed after the formatter change.
- Removed the temporary test as requested.
- Re-ran tuple formatter tests:

```text
cargo +nightly test -p gleam-core format::tests::tuple
```

Result:

```text
13 passed; 0 failed
```

Notes:
- I did not commit changes.
- `compiler-core/src/format/tests/tuple.rs` and `Cargo.lock` are restored/no longer changed.
- There were pre-existing unrelated working tree changes in:
  - `compiler-core/src/erlang.rs`
  - `compiler-core/src/type_/expression.rs`
  - `hexpm/src/lib.rs`
  - untracked `reports/`

Only `compiler-core/src/format.rs` was intentionally modified for this bug.
